name: Kinsta Managed WordPress Hosting API
what_it_does: Managed WordPress hosting platform on Google Cloud with REST API — enabling WordPress developers and agencies to programmatically manage WordPress sites, environments, domains, CDN (Cloudflare Enterprise), and deployments via API, with all sites running on Google Cloud C2 instances for high-performance hosting.
use_cases: Automating WordPress site provisioning via Kinsta API for agency client onboarding
Managing staging environments and deployments via Kinsta API for CI/CD workflows
Retrieving site analytics and performance data via Kinsta API for monitoring automation
Managing domains and SSL via Kinsta API for WordPress fleet management
Integrating Kinsta site data into dashboards via API for agency reporting
not_for: Budget WordPress hosting (Kinsta is premium — use SiteGround or A2 for lower cost)
Application hosting beyond WordPress (use Kinsta Application Hosting separately)
best_when: A WordPress-focused agency or developer needs high-performance Google Cloud WordPress hosting with API automation — where Kinsta provides Google C2 instances, Cloudflare Enterprise CDN, and a developer-friendly API for site fleet management.
avoid_when: You need non-WordPress hosting, budget hosting, or are not a WordPress-focused developer or agency.

auth.notes: Bearer token authentication. API key generated in MyKinsta dashboard. Authorization: Bearer {api-key} header. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Company-level and site-level access via single API key. No webhooks documented. API access included in all plans.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $35/month (1 site); Pro $70/month (2 sites); Business $115-475/month (5-40 sites); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Hungarian company (Budapest, 2013). Founded by Mark Gavalics and Peter Nemeth. Bootstrapped (no VC funding as of 2024). 25,000+ customers. Google Cloud Platform (GCP) powered. C2 compute-optimized instances. Cloudflare Enterprise CDN included. Free SSL, automatic daily backups, staging. LXD containers (one per site). Redis caching. PHP 8.x. No-lock-in migration tools. Kinsta also offers: Application Hosting (non-WordPress), Static Site Hosting, Database Hosting. Notable: bootstrapped profitable company with strong developer reputation.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; not fully documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with Bearer token. SOC2/GDPR. Google Cloud backend. Cloudflare Enterprise CDN. No webhooks. WordPress-only managed hosting. Good developer documentation. API included in all plans.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Site/environment reads idempotent by site ID. Domain reads idempotent. Site creation not idempotent — check for existing before creating. Deployment operations not idempotent — verify state first.
known_agent_gotchas: WordPress-only managed hosting — no way to host non-WordPress apps on WordPress plans
Site ID (UUID) is primary identifier — retrieve from API or MyKinsta dashboard
No webhooks — must poll API for deployment status and environment changes
API key has company-wide access — no granular per-site key scoping
Kinsta Application Hosting (non-WordPress) has a separate API and product line
Staging environments are clones — pushing staging to production is one-way and permanent

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, GDPR compliant. Bearer token auth. No OAuth2 scope granularity. Google Cloud infrastructure security. Cloudflare Enterprise DDoS protection. LXD containers (isolation per site). EU data residency available. Bootstrapped company — security-conscious culture. No webhooks (reduces attack surface).
